text: worst customer service ever. the guy on the other end of the phone is barely understandable. and all they do is dance around your issues with non-helpful questions and bs.

I would not ever recommend going to T mobile!! The customer service is the worst of the worst and nobody will do anything to help resolve issues. I purchased a phone on 25Nov, received notification from UPS that my package was lost I was told that I should be refunded by the 6th, that didn't happen, I was then told I would receive my refund by the 14th, that didn't happen and am now being told it's not going to be resolved until the 29th. There is clearly a lack of communication in this organization and the fact it's taking over a month to fulfill or refund orders is horrible. I will be switching to AT&T as soon as this order is refunded. Whenever that will be.

DO NOT DO IT! WORST COMPANY EVER. GOT THEIR WIFI UNDER THE 15 DAY MONEY BACK GUARANTEE AND SENT THE EQUIPMENT BACK WITHIN 7 days and they sent me to collections for $112….

Tried calling them and they said the 6 digit pin I gave them was incorrect and there’s nothing they can do for me. I even offered my Social Security number and they refused….

switched from ATT.  From day 1, most calls get dropped and more than 50% of text messages won't go through.    After 12 FULL hours with Tmobile support, they could not figure out how to make their service work.   I then asked chatGPT and it said to check if I was using Google messages.  Sure enough, the employee at the store had installed and set that up as my text app WITHOUT my permission.   I deleted the Google message app and Text messaging is better, now at least 80%, but still some are being missed and/or delayed significantly.  We tested it is not location based.  Tried connecting at 5+ cell towers with 5 bars and still an issue.   
Switching back to ATT.

I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile around 8 years ago because Verizon customer service was horrid.

I've never had a problem with them until recently, they refused our jump status which we pay for because we didn't want to pay more for the new plan. So therefore what I'm paying for is bull!!!!
Then I got the Internet tower and they stated that price would not go up from $40 month.  I recently saw my bill and Internet was $45 a month.🤬

Walked into T-mobile 1491 W Mason St, Green Bay, WI 54303 with a phone I bought offline ready to buy a plan and start service.  The two employees there never acknowledged me so I took a seat and waited for a half hour while the employees continued to pretend I didn't exist so I finally left.  The next day, my husband took the phone there and the same guy who was working yesterday claimed he couldn't hook this phone up with service, he was completely aloof and very unhelpful.  I took the phone to a different T-mobile store a few blocks away and they hooked it up, no problem.  That location is notoriously subpar and I wonder how it stays open with those kind of employees.

signed up for home internet never worked properly. (spotty at best) Contacted them 4 separate times, no better. wanted to cancel had to complete billing cycle. charged for somthing that never worked as promised.

If I could leave a negative Zero rating for Tmobile I would. Customer service is horrible they have to be the most incompetent people I have EVER COME ACROSS Down to my most recent conversation with a manager named John!! If he is in charge I understand why they can get it right. I asked to cancel my service on November 26 its December 7th and my lines are still on. I advised sprint's service was crappy but customer service was so much better his response oh we won't do what you want no sir I want you to do what is right I work in a collection environment I don't believe that the customer is always right but I do stand on principles and excellent customer service and TMobile has NEITHER!! Do yourself a favor buy a phone LITERALLY ANYWHERE ELSE BUT HERE save yourself from the JOHN'S and Tmobile unfilled agreements!

Went to T-Mobile store in Sept 2023. Brought my parents to my account and was given "free" phones, as a promotion.   Was told to verify with T-Mobile regarding billing.  When i did so in September,  I was told I would see the charge for the phone, but would also see a credit for that same amount.  So here it is December and I'm being billed for the phone with no credit.  Called T-Mobile.  The rep stated there was no promotion on "their" end, and there were no notes on my account regarding a call regarding a credit,  and they were sorry someone told me I would get a credit.  I asked who would review the recording of that previous conversation, I was told to go to the store (an hour away) to try and resolve my problem.
Horrible company. hidden fees.

On 12-06-23, my mother and I walked in to the T-mobile store located at 1491 W Mason St, Green Bay, WI 54303 to buy a phone and start a plan.  We walked in and we were never greeted at all, no "hey", no "we'll be with you in a minute", nothing.  We took a seat and waited for a half hour but were never even acknowledged in the least so finally, exasperated and a bit shocked, we got up and left.  Those two T-Mobile workers in there were the two most rude, unqualified airheads I've ever personally experienced in a retail setting.  Their customer service, or lack thereof, was horrible and virtually nonexistent.  Even a dog will come up and greet someone, these people could not have ignored us more.
bad tmobile
IF THERE WAS A 0 START THATS WHAT I WOULD GIVE THEY ARE THE WORST I HAVE EVER HAD

text: Horrible! Don’t get involved with these crooks. Signed up on a “promotion” to buy a phone for 400.00. I didn’t need 24 months to pay off 278.00. They said if I wanted to leave my phone would now cost me 800.00. These guys are thieves and thugs

Don't ever get tmobile phone service, employees do not do their job right, called to cancel my service and one line didn't get canceled, which was negligence on their part, they were continuing to charge my account, even though I had canceled my lines, called various times ti fix this problem. Legal advice will be sought. Sooo make sure that they cancel all lines and not take their word for it.
They changed billing to paperless without letting customers know. Now it costs $1.99 to get a detailed bill in the mail! That's absolutely insane.

T-Mobile literally stranded us abroad and proceeded to extort us and lie to us. We upgraded to iPhone15 Pro's and changed our plan with a salesperson in South Florida. We have a second line on our phones which we use when we are in second home in Ecuador. The salesperson said we would simply need to change to an esim to install it. We tried that, but the phones are locked, and T-Mobile does not do temporary unlocks for travel anymore. After calling and working through their process, Janica (a Supervisor), told us if we paid off the phones in full ($2k), she would get them unlocked for us. We did. Seventy-two hours later (the latest she said it would take), they are not unlocked. We cannot access our bank down here without secondary text message on our Ecuadorian line; we cannot access our healthcare plan or communicate with our physicians, etc. A new CS rep said she would put in a request and it would take 24-72 hours more.  Still not done-- T-Mobile's service is reprehensible.

Tried to order watches for Christmas gifts for my kids. Took 5 sales people and 1 managers and they still got the order wrong. When I called t mobile in Wyandotte I was told for me to just go online and order the bands myself or call the number they can’t help me. I’m talking being on hold trying to place the order for over 10hrs total and the order comes wrong. Now after sitting on hold asking for new bands to be sent to me for two hours. I’m waiting for a manager for another hour or two to try to correct the problem. ￼

We upgraded our devices to the iPhone 15 Pro with dual e-sim slots and upgraded our service plans accordingly to Go5G Plus which would make is eligible for upgrades every 2 yrs and with the promo would pay off the phones over those 2 yrs. However, if you wanted to take advantage of the second e-sim slot e.g. for international travel to be able to use foreign networks for faster data and calls while abroad, you couldn’t. If you needed a second e-sim you have to have the phone paid outright AND the device must be used with T-mobile for at least 40 days according to T-mobile’s current policies. It’s not an issue if you added a second T-mobile line to the phone but good luck if you want to use it for travel outside Can or Mex. If you stick to the promo where T-mobile eventually pays off the cost of the phone over 2 years (via monthly credits), you would never be able to access the second e-sim unless you held on to your now 2yr old phone and chose not to upgrade. We have spent hours with customer service trying to explain our needs so that we can use the second e-sim slot: we paid off 2 iPhone 15s, we even said they could re-lock the phones after we added our foreign e-sim. Only under the threats of leaving T-mobile did we get contacts from higher level reps who have now promised us twice that they would unlock the phones. At the time of this review, we have still not gotten any results or updates within the timeframe of their “personal guarantees.” Meanwhile, we are living abroad with less than optimum T-mobile service (using their local partner network). Some local businesses and services we use here require a local number which we have (because we had local plans before we switched devices and plans) but can no longer use or access them since we can’t add the second e-sim. 

TLDR: International travelers beware. T-Mobile policies don’t make things easy. You can’t use the phone to its full potential. Customer service has not kept its promises.

I traveled recently and had an amazing experience with the data and connection in other countries with my T-Mobile service. It helped so much! I used to have sprint and traveled at that time and the connection wasn't good at all so this was a huge relief. I tried to file a compliment with T-Mobile but couldn't find an option online so I'm writing the review here.
I cancelled my service and have been waiting on a refund for 4 months. Each time I call I have to start from begining.
Love T-Mobile!! Been with them over 2 yrs and never had a problem. Great customer service each and every time. Great coverage and they have a great internet...

I been with Tmobile for 21 years and now all of a sudden I'm having phone and billing issues,  I had the flip phone z4 or whatever an they said that I could trade it in for whatever phone I wanted,  I wanted the iPhone 15 of course they wouldn't have it until 3 months later so my ass got talked into the Samsung ultra 23. Had the phone now for a month and now it's a billing issue saying I couldn't trade the phone in due to I didn't pay the phone off,  a whole month, so the customer service representative saying I understand,  no the HELL YOU DON'T,  I wanted money taken off from buying a new phone. Tmobile SUCK ASS yes I'm mad

I've been with Sprint for 11 years.  I have a business account with T Mobile and was expecting a smooth transition. however, I've only experienced horror. One of my phone lines returned in July a phone because of a warranty issue. sprint contacted T-Mobile to tell them that the phone was returned.  you would think that they would credit my account the $1,00+ fee. no way!!! they have disconnected my phone line over 16 times and threatened to permanently disconnect my phone because of a phone I no longer have.  after three supervisors and over 8 hours of customer service phone time there has been no resolution.  why is every time I call I have to retell my story from the beginning.  do they have notes are anything?  please give me my number so I can go to a real cell phone company.  I'm contemplating filing a lawsuit for lost revenue.

I started having issues not receiving my phone calls!!! My business is ran off my cell phone.  The technician at tmobile literally told me that the phone is junk. That every customer with the new s23s are all having these same issues!!!! GO TO VERIZON AND GET A GOOGLE PHONE OR A IPHONE. YOUR WELCOME.

horrible. the manager refunds my account after I was told the money he refunded wouldn't be taken out. now they expect me to pay the money back for the money the manager refunded. they even told me to ask a family member to help me get the money, how rude is that. you really think they would have know not to tell a customer something like this. then after I told them I was leaving because they are horrible and help. they have the nerve to try and send a review on how my experience was. They need todo better with their customer service department.
Purchased a iPhone from them and they will not unlock my device have call them for the last 3 weeks they keep telling me it’s unlocked and it isn’t been without my phone for 3weeks

Will never do business with t mobile again and will make sure everyone knows about our horrible experience. Not only was our coverage way worse than AT&T, but we also had a few customer services issues with them throughout our time. However, by far the worst was when we decided to leave them. 
Back story…when we decided to sign up with them they said “we’ll throw on a third line because there is a deal and it’s free anyways. You don’t ever have to use it but can if you decide down the line you’d like to” this was fine obviously. However when we cancelled we had forgotten about the third line and nobody mentioned it. The problem came when t mobile continued to charge us for our full bill…when we called they apologized and stated they would refund us. However the following month came around and we never received a refund and were charged AGAIN for a line we literally don’t even have active. Again we call and they apologized and finally refunded us the $280. 
This was until we were charged AGAIN…the people were never actually canceling the line. We went into the store and they now we’re saying we’d have to pay $140 for our “last month” again for a phone line we NEVER USED. 
We called again and nobody seems to want to resolve this despite telling us twice on the phone we would be refunded  and it was a mistake. To out of nowhere be like “you need to pay a last bill” after months of us contacting them is unacceptable.
We will likely end up paying this bill for a phone line we never used.
We absolutely without a doubt will never recommend t mobile to anyone and in fact we believe they will cheat you out of money anyway they can. Not worth it…definitely not worth it.

I hate this company. I was not explained when I traded in my phone that I was agreeing to being stuck with the company for 24 months. The chat and phone agents are constantly giving misinformation. They have not been able to send me a copy of my contract they keep sending me a link that takes me to my profile dashboard. Their website sucks and their mobile app is even worse. A chat agent promised me that my phone was going to get unlocked in 72 hrs if I paid $142.94 because that is all that I owed on my device. 72 hrs later I called and they tell me the agent lied to me. They are applying a trade in promotion in 24 months for a trade in device they ALREADY RECEIVED! I am not sending them pieces of my trade in each month for 24 months. On top of that if I want out of the company they are asking me to pay over $400 dollars when I have already given them my previous device and got a credit for almost $800, I have been paying installments for many months and last Saturday a chat agent told me all I had to do was pay $142.94 if you all of that up it is $1001 for a phone that’s worth $1000 but because I want to leave the company because I hate it and I am unhappy they want to charge over $400 as punishment but they call it “promotion” for a device they already received. They shouldn’t force me to stay with them if I don’t want to. They also can’t give me a better price and I have been asking for months. I hate Tmobile and I will make sure I let every single person I talk to know about the horrible experience, how toxic they are forcing me to stay with them when I don’t want to, how much they’ve lied to me, how bad their website and mobile app is and how they want to steal money from me when I already paid my device in full. Even car loans allow you to pay your loan early. Tmobile would rather have a million unhappy customers against their will than let people leave the company without hating them. I HATE TMOBILE!

After over 13 Years, NO ONE deserves this treatment. I asked for a more economical rate (July) they didn't provide a solution that could beat Xfinity mobile so I left. T-Mobile closed my access to my account immediately online. They began sending me bills and charged me from July, August, & September. I called to dispute charges stating that I wasn't a customer and wouldn't pay. The rep claimed they would cover to cost but I had to pay up until July-August; and all other charges would be dropped by T-mobile. I asked for written confirmation and didn't receive it. Fast forward to today, I have a new bill claiming $7.00 late fee for the remaining balance of over $120 which isn't included in the bill. Later, I receive a debt communication letter from a debt collector for the $120+ amount for a bill on November 9th. Now, how am I billed on November 9th and then sent to a debt collector within 2 weeks, I have no idea. I used to love them for the rates....but this has been a complete nightmare.....I WOULD NEVER RECOMMEND THEM TO ANYONE.

T-Mobile, is a very shady place, been trying for over a month to get my refund, talked to about 10 customer service people and have been getting the run around, think twice before dealing with the clowns in customer service, I never been so pissed off, never would recommend these people to anyone ,Oct 4th and still no refund as of today Nov 18

Obviously he took it personal when I told him I had change all my lines to another company (I thought he was aware of) He, changed his mood immediately. The rep that help me to close my account (Juan, very nice guy) told me I will have a refund, but Carlos said I did not explain to them that I was closing the account, (it was obvious when they gave me the code to unlock the phones and switch companies) and  I will not receive a refund. When the iPhone 15 came out, T-Mobile gave a great offer. I was doubtful of their coverage in my area, and the salesperson told if there were any issues with coverage I could return the devices in 14 days totally free of charge. "There will be no charges at all." When the phone arrived, I could not even make a call from my home due to the bad signal. Made one call from outside to check and a few text messages (8 total in the USA), and used 36 MB of data to check the internet speed while outside. Found the signal was horrible in our area and returned all 3 devices! (Did not even open one packet). After a month and a half, I got a bill for $257.39. When called the support center at Phillipense, they told me I owed this bill and they could not do anything, as this is an automated bill. Spoke to the manager who said you have to pay or it will go to the collection agency. He also stated they cannot help with the bill as it is automated. I wondered why they have customer service for billing. Anyway, I paid the bill while with the manager who said the bill is only around $48 for 4-day charges for the lines they sent to test. I was using my Verizon phone all these times, glad I did not cancel it. After 4 days of paying the bill, got a letter from a collection agency telling me I owed T-Mobile $257.39. Can't believe this, as they never even sent me a first-time bill, and I had given my Credit for them to monthly charge me, which got canceled by them when they stopped my service. This was a horrible experience for me, and I would strongly suggest you keep away from testing their services, as they are going to charge you for the days. Don't believe the salesperson.

My only option is to buy a new phone that is Samsung.  This is crazy!!  Why are they selling the watch that does not work with my phone.  They then will not take the watch back.

Tmobile has one of the most disrespectful and incompetent employees, especially the call center located in India.  These individuals provide fake names, they love to hear themselves speak, lack listening and communication skills and have no customer service skills.  It appears that they use your call to practice their English.  The corporate office where one should be available to file a complaint, the representative transfers the call to a B.S. department, when you call back and request to speak to a supervisor, you are told that you are being reported for harassment and your call is blocked.  This is Tmobile mission statement- to provide disrespectful agents and a disservice, while you pay us.

T-Mobile has been a hassle , I had gotten the high speed internet because the lady at the store said it would be $35 a month on my next bill it said I added a phone line and charged me $45 for the internet then I just paid my phone bill last month I had $26.25 left on my phone and I paid it and I looked on my account they never took that payment off so I'm again going to pay for a phone that should of been paid off last month.

Back in about 2009 T-Mobile sold my personal information. As my contract was coming to an end I was contacted by phone by someone asking if I wanted to renew my contract. I went ahead assuming it was T-Mobile at the time, but it turned out to be '3' Mobile and I didn't realise they had sold my details until I read about it, and being naive about mobile phone companies, I thought that '3' were part of T-mobile. I only found out about the data being sold by reading about it in the papers at the time. Needless to say I haven't used T-mobile nor '3' mobile ever since. However, we are now in 2023 and I'm still receiving scam and spam emails from all the usual culprits at the rate of some 15-20 a day. It is so brazen that they always quote a reference number and guess what? It is is the last 9 digits of that original T-Mobile number I had so there is no escaping who is to blame. Beware they will sell your personal details! Also '3' Mobile need a mention too as they bought those stolen details, so don't trust them either. I've left this for so long but after all this time I've finally had enough and the fallout from a company that still drags on. Someone less aware will get stung no doubt further by the scam emails proliferation. I would have liked to have given no stars to both those companies

Worst experience ever, they promised me 800$ in credit in an offer, then told me I didn’t qualified so all they would give was 400$ for my trade in, now that I want to pay whatever is left on my phone after a year they’re telling me my trade in was worth only 80$ bucks and the 320$ they “gave me” was the promotion which I wouldn’t qualified if I paid my phone before my 2 years contract, what a bullsh!t, they basically got my phone for free, worst scammers ever

My family and I went to TMobile on Centerville Rd because of a phone issue. Scott and Harry were the best! Both were so helpful and knowledgeable about the products, the promotions the bill, etc. they really took their time to explain things to us and help us find the best plan for our family. I would HIGHLY recommend this store to everyone. Great customer service!!

I have had T-Mobile 5G Home Internet for 11 months. It was traumatic to install and took many hours. The Gateway is a small corded box controlled by a mobile based app, just plug the box in and turn on. My T-Mobile tower is 1/2 mile from my house, my Gateway sits next to a window about 10-30 feet from my devices, a MacAir book, 3 ipads, 2 iphones, an Epson printer USB connected to the Air book, and an LG smart tv. The advertised download speed is true, between 100-400 bps.
The app, once the complicated passwords are renamed, is easy to use.
My connection shows 'Excellent' about 95% of the time. I assume that means the connection between the box and the tower. The connection between the devices and the gateway are sporadic. Two of the ipads, uncorded, disconnect at least five times during the day, the worst is the lost connection to the 'Air Printer' several times a week. What is strange the one ipad and the tv are 100% corded and never lose connection. I suspect sharing the tower with the T-Mobile cellphone users may be part of the problem. My few calls to customer service were helpful and quick. I have paperless auto pay and is no problem. I have not disconnected the gateway and moved it to another location in the town, so can't say how that would work out. I recommend the service, which has limited locations, for the convenience, price, and listed speed, Maybe you would not experience the disconnect problems I have, a nuisance, but easily fixed by 'forgetting the network' and reconnecting with the password, There is no contract so a tryout will be easy to give up if not satisfied.

Customer service was horrible.  My main screen went out and I followed the instructions for resets and even went to the store they recommended to run diagnostics on the phone which said the screen was defective.  I called back to get a replacement and the tech wanted me to to repeat everything again and go to a T mobile store.  The rep the day before said they would ship a new phone since I have insurance.
Spent two hours on the phone. Very disappointed this was not resolved and will not be held hostage for two hours at a store like I was when I switched from ATT.  Buyer beware

We had to cancel our t-mobile internet because the service was awful in our area. I was told I would get an email with a shipping label attached to return the router.  I received the email but with no shipping label attached. Tonight I spent two hours on hold and still don’t have an email with a shipping label. The last person that I spoke with told me I would have it within three hours. That was six hours ago, so I assume I won’t receive it. I believe this is t-mobile’s way of being able to charge the customer for non-returned equipment.  Long story short……buyer beware.
The people at the T Mobile store were so friendly and helpful.

The customer service for TMobile internet has been absolutely fantastic. Our internet has been wonderful since we switched from our previous company (Spectrum). Also when I got stuck setting up my online account today, Robert, was a huge help in setting up my online account. Thank you Robert for the wonderful customer service that you provided!
5 star for Amir and Ashley of T-mobile in. 405 West Genesee St., Camillus NY.  Excellent service. Thank you guys.

I would give them zero stars if I could. Switched 6 weeks ago and totally regret it. Customer service is not knowledgeable. My employer discount would not be applied even though they said at the beginning it would. I will stay though my phone trade in credits then leave this horrendous company. Beware.

I went to a T-mobile store in Bellevue PA and was promised one thing and then after I paid money, said that they wouldn't honor their offer and that I had to pay more after I confirmed the terms of the offer many times.  When I called T-mobile for help, they promised I would receive calls back from the store and from a supervisor but no calls ever came.  I called 4 times with the same promise each time. Every time I called, I got different information.  I am looking to move to a new carrier now.

My phone was lost, I had insurance and wanted to use it. It took a chatbot and three phones to get the phone mailed to me because Custer service wouldn't give me the information needed to send the replacement phone. When I received the phone in the mail it took an hour and a half at the store to activate the sim card. The 800 number customer service would not help us with the one time pin. I was told there was nothing they could do and asked to leave the store. When I refused until they activated my phone id waited 30 additional minute until it was done. T Mobile CSR are indecent.

Just got a text, if the payment method is cc, you will jot get the auto payment discount. How low can this company go, just the greed at the C suite level is horrendous. They are not cheaper than anyone else, just a bad bad company.

T-Mobile’s in-store customer service left me feeling frustrated and disappointed. I visited the store to replace my internet router, but the staff refused to help me, claiming that the router was defective and that replacing it would hurt their numbers. They told me I would have to go to corporate or mail it in and wait for it to be shipped. I explained that I needed internet right away and couldn’t be without it for several days, but they still refused to help. I have a business account with 12 lines, but they didn’t seem to care about my needs. The customer service specialist was rude and unhelpful, and I was left without internet for several days.
I would not recommend this store to anyone. The in-store customer service is terrible, and the staff doesn’t seem to care about their customers’ needs. I plan to reach out to T-Mobile’s corporate team and management to file a complaint about this store and its poor customer service. I hope that T-Mobile will take steps to improve its in-store customer service so that other customers don’t have to go through the same frustrating experience that I did.

Stay away from this company their billing practices boarder on criminal!  My closing date for my account was 10/11/23.  They charge ahead for their services so when I canceled on 10/12/23 I figured I would be refunded for all $60 - less 1 or 2 days.  Today I get another bill for $60(11/4/23)!  I called T-Mobile billing & was told "yes bill is pay ahead.  Because I did not cancel before start of new cycle I am responsible for the full month"!!!  What type of customer service is this! Now I am paying double for my service, because new & better customer service company Consumer Cellular, charges after use!
Again STAY AWAY FROM TMOBILE THEY ARE NOT A REPUTABLE COMPANY PUNISH YOU FOR DECONTINUING THEY PLANS!!!

Don't go to tmobile, they are liars and don't know what information they are advertising!  I had an account with ATT for 10+ years and I was getting charged over $400/month for tv, internet, and phone.  I went up to midtown plaza to tmobile when the store was there to switch my provider.  Gabby, the store manager, assisted me.  She told me I could switch over to t-mobile for a lot cheaper for all services and I could trade my ATT phones and they would be all paid off, in full.  I had two phones from ATT, an iphone and an android that I traded in.  No further instruction was given, they said they would take care of it, and received a confirmation email of the same!  4 months later, I am getting a bill from ATT that I owe for the phones that were switched over, over $1000.00!  I kept calling Gabby and she kept saying she would look into it.  After several months, now I am in collections and my credit that I worked so hard to get back up, is ruined.  Apparently, Gabby, keep in mind she is a manager, had the wrong information.  Because I was on a payment plan, the phones were not eligible for this program!  Shouldn't a manger know this?  After all the back and forth, and finally finding this out, btw, noone knew this info up front, I attempted to call Gabby again, but this time, no response.  Why?  Because she was wrong, made a mistake and she couldn't fix it.  First of all, if you are a manager, you should know the policy and how the programs work.  Then, when you made the mistake, you fix it one way or another!  It didn't matter to her, she made her money and I am in collections ruining my good credit!  This shouldn't be allowed.  I was a nurse and if I gave someone the wrong info, guess what, people could lose their lives.  Obviously not the same thing, but the main point is, is that if you have a job, you do it, you do it well, you do it right or you get fired or you are screwing someone else's finances or lives or what not.  DO NOT go to tmobile, they dont have the correct information or everyone isn't on the same page!  All they care about is making their profit.  This shouldn't be allowed.  There is NO excuse!!!! A manager like this does not deserve to be a manager!  I will be cancelling my account, they don't even care to lose a customer.  Oh well, maybe that's why the store that I originally went to closed, they SUCK.  Have all the information, paperwork, etc you need when you open account with anyone.  Because when you get screwed in the end, you better have your ducks in a row because they change up their story to cover their asses.  I know she will get this review and I hope her manager fires her for the crappy manager she is!

In my opinion, T-Mobile is the worst customer service I have ever dealt with. Sprint was transferred to Tmobile. I’ve noticed that anytime I call it’s always a 2 to 3 hour ordeal to get anything fixed, that’s if they fix it at all.
My advice to anyone switching to a different plan is be prepared to pay quite a bit of money because they do not keep track of the account that you’re transferring your plan from. They don’t even know how to look it up. We switched from our business account to a 55+ account and without our knowledge they put it into a plus premium which charged us $60 more a month. Took hours to get that fixed. Also, after switching the account to the 55+ plan I paid it immediately but little to my knowledge did I know that the previous plan we were on had also billed us  Which is OK but they did not tell us about it and I found out that it was going to be turned over to collection. Tmobile needs to put all their billing access together rather than separately. When switching over the account, they did not tell us that we still owed money on our pre-contracted phones, which caused my phone to be locked so I could not even transfer to a different service without going through all the hassle of calling over and over. When I found out that we owed money for our contract phones, I immediately paid that and it still took hours and hours and hours of calling to get my phone unlocked, I am no longer a T-Mobile customer, but I would say that is the worst customer service that I have ever had to deal with. I would not recommend TMobile to anyone.

I have two choices for internet where I live. The only other service is constantly broken, and does not provide even the 'guaranteed' 20Mb/s they promise. T-Mobile REFUSES TO HONOR any Affordable Connectivity Program (federal) discounts. They do not tell you this upfront, and they do not offer any other options. You cannot even lodge an official complaint with anyone. If you are disabled, and every dollar counts, DO NOT SWITCH TO T-Mobile. They DO NOT CARE ABOUT PEOPLE.

5G wireless for $50 sounds great, right? And guaranteed services! T-mobile wifi drops every time a fighter jet or plain flying over my house! Every day at random times their router would turn off and reboot constantly. After seeking T-mobile assistance for weeks they "had no idea why their router" kept messing up. Everyone from the services department kept saying how amazing the speed was???  Again the speed was not an issue. Not having an internet connection that they guaranteed was the problem. How is the router dropping off and on all day long when they had two 5G towers in front and back of our house? T-Mobile did NOT account for the most extensive Air Force Base, and their air runways are directly over us. Hello, the Air Force Fighter Jets have the equipment to block radio waves/5G! We never had a single person from T-Mobile help. Their guaranteed wifi was a lie. We paid them for months with their guarantees they could solve this issue; ⚠️ We Kept getting the runaround. I was distraught and never refunded anything even though I was guaranteed service🤬 Do NOT get their 5G!!!

ABSOLUTE NIGHTMARE! T-Mobile uses Assurant Insurance, which has their customer service department in the Phillipines. They do not speak English well. They are super disorganized. I spent more than 5 HOURS ON THE PHONE trying to process a damaged PHONE CLAIM. Even the supervisors would hang up on me and not call me back. They even shipped me a label that was inactive so I was informed by the USPS that because the Assurant shipping label did not work I had to pay to ship the device. I contacted Assurant and they said that they would not reimburse me. The agent said that they do not have a Tax ID number. This has to be the MOST INCOMPETENT COMPANY that I have ever come across. Choose a cel phone carrier that does not use Assurant Insurance. It is extremely stressful and time consuming to process claims with them.

Approximately 4 years ago I purchased a 2 galaxy watches with T-Mobile. one of the watches was damaged and T-Mobile exchanged it for another watch and that required opening another account. Our old phone carrier shut down so we were forced to change carriers. I went to a T-Mobile store in Maize, KS and Maria, the lady that helped me, told me we would definitely have 5G coverage where we live. I questioned her about that several times during our interaction because my husband and I live very rural and when I looked at the coverage map before heading into the store, coverage looked spotty. She kept telling me we would FOR SURE have 5G coverage. I didn't purchase a new phone until I knew it would work at our house. After a couple weeks, I decided to purchase a new device with them because we at least had 4G coverage. Then, shortly after the 14 day return period expired, our service is almost nonexistent. I called customer service and the guy I spoke to informed me that he would have never sold us a plan and phones because his map showed almost no coverage where we live. He told me that we basically get "left over" signals from a tower that is very far away from us and the reason we don't have service now is because they were having problems with said tower. I asked when the tower was going to be up and he said he didn't know. Now, we don't get service, thank goodness we have wifi or my new expensive phone (that they won't let me pay off early or return) would be a paper weight. The only way out from under them now is to pay off mine and my husband's devices at full retail pricing, not just the sale pricing. My husband's phone hasn't even left the box, but we can't return it. I asked the customer service guy if there's anything we can do since we don't have service and he said, "No, nothing." So basically we have to pay ridiculous prices to them but our phones only work on wifi, that we also have to pay for. I'm extremely disappointed with T-Mobile. We'll be switching to Verizon as soon as we're able to.

I've been a TMobile customer for over 20 years, since it was Aerial, back in 1997.
 
For more than 6 months, I've been paying late fees because my payment due date was originally the 13th of each month and I usually make my payment when I get paid on the 15th of each month. In August, I contacted TMobile Customer Service  to request my payment due date to be changed to at least the 15th of each month. For my surprise, after I was informed that the change was made, the following bill (September) was still reflecting the 10th as the due date for the payments. I patiently contacted TMobile Customer Service once again and supposedly, the issue was resolved, but I still had to pay the late charges. When I received the notification for the October payment, once again, the due date reflected is the 10th. I contacted TMobile again and I was given a credit so ok. I was also explained that I could schedule a payment on October 15th or 16th, as a payment arrangement, and that the November payment was to be due as requested, on or after the 15th of each month. For my surprise, and once again, I just received the notification about my bill with a due date of 11/10th and this time the total amount due is $20 higher than the last amount paid.
 
I contacted customer service once again, this time yelling and screaming to whom identified himself as a Supervisor, Brandon, who stated that he was able to change the due date but I will still be responsible to pay the November bill on the 10th. He offered a $20 credit (A JOKE, AN INSULT) as damage control. Ridiculous.
 
I'm not asking for $20.  I'm asking to get the due date fixed. I feel it is unfair that after over 20 years of paying on time, I need to keep paying late fees due to the incompetency of the TMobile's staff, where nobody seems to be able to make a simple change to an account. As a mortgage loan processor, I know the importance of paying on time; however, looks like TMobile is unwilling to help in order to keep collecting late fees.

On October 7, 2023 I went to a T-Mobile store located at 100 Viewmont Mall 842, Scranton, PA 18508. I went into the store and HUNTER helped me. Hunter told me about a promotion and a trade in value. Hunter asserted to me and multiple other people including the account owner that the promotion they were running was for $350.00 PLUS IN ADDITION the trade in value of the old phone which was valued at $260.00 for a total of $610.00 being applied to the new phone making the installment payments a total of $24.58 monthly. On October 12, 2023 the phone was received and I was to do the deferred trade in for the old phone. On October 14, 2023, I spoke to a representative named Jennifer over the phone (recorded calls) and explained the situation, she promised me a call back Monday October 16, 2023. THIS CALL NEVER OCCURRED BECAUSE SHE NEVER CALLED ME On October 16, 2023 after failure by Jennifer to call me back I called again and was transferred to Nadia another rep. I explained the situation to her and because of the situation she sent me to her Supervisor Danielle. Danielle said that there was nothing they could do to honor the price the EMPLOYEE gave me on October 7, 2023. T-mobile's employee misrepresented the terms of the promotion to MULTIPLE PEOPLE IN RELIANCE THE MISREPRESENTED TERMS THE AGREEMENT WAS AGREED TO TMOBILE DOES NOT WANT TO TAKE ACCOUNTABILITY FOR THERE OWN EMPLOYEE'S ERRORS. To put into context, there was misinformation provided by TMOBILE'S EMPLOYEE who they TRAIN and who they are RESPONSIBLE FOR. We had T-Mobile internet service for 19 months.  The T-Mobile service at the beginning was pretty good, with the service freezing needing a restart only about once a week. However, the towards the end in August of 2023, the gateway(modem) needed to be restarted several times a day in order to function. Around the same time T-Mobile removed the $5 discount for auto pay if you were using a credit card. Something they had allowed for over a year. On Aug 23rd we ordered Xfinity. The Xfinity gateway would be delivered in several days. On Aug 24th, 2023 I called customer service and told them I wished to cancel. However, customer service said I needed to try a new gateway first to see if that would correct the problem. I figured I would compare them both. T-Mobile sent a replacement quickly, but it did not do much better with Mbps download of 44.  The Xfinity gateway came a few days later with Mbps in the 400's for downloads without any problems. I canceled T-Mobile service on Sept 2, 23 through their customer service. When canceling they requested the 6 digit number that I gave them when I signed up 19 months earlier.  I gave them the number I had written down in my T-Mobile file. The customer service rep said it was the wrong number. I said that is the one I uses. They said it was imperative that I give them the 6-digit code.  I asked if they could use my soc sec number or email me a reset and they said no. They said that I would have to physically go to a T-Mobile store and request my 6-digit number after they verified my identity.  I drove to the nearest store located at 2024 IL-50 Ste A1, Bourbonnais IL 60914 and spoke with a rep named Michael.  He said he could not give me the 6-digit number and told me to call customer service back and tell them so. I went home and called and told them what he said, and they said okay and that they would email a return label for the gateway.  I received the email with the label on September 2nd, 2023 and returned the gateway to UPS. T-Mobile received the gateway on Sept 11 23.  I received a letter stating that "They received the gateway, and no further action is needed on your part." Then on Sept 19, 23 T-Mobile collected an auto pay of $55 from my credit card on file.  I called my credit card to dispute the charge.  T-Mobile sent me a new bill asking for the $55, plus $25 for a chargeback, Plus the next months $55 for a total of $135.  I called customer service on Oct 7, 23, and they said I needed to go to an actual store because they could not help me again because of the 6-digit pin saying the one I gave them was incorrect. I went to the store in Bradley Il and they told me that the account had never been canceled, even though customer service sent the return label and had received the gateway. The store contacted T-mobile customer service from their store and eventually a very rude corporate manager named Patrick said that they insisted I pay the full $135.  I explained I had cancelled the service on Sept 2, 23 and returned the gateway.  He said it was T-Mobiles' policy to have you also physically come to the store to cancel and that it wasn't officially canceled until that day which was over a month later. I told him I physically went to the T-Mobile store but Michael would not give me the pin.  He scolded me saying I should have used the phrase "I wish to have you cancel my account" I explained that I had already done that with customer service rep on the phone and that I had told Michael that I needed the pin number for them to cancel it. Patrick from Corporate says that I am repeating myself and it was my fault for not saying that I wanted Michael to cancel the account.  He then said that he would offer me a one-time deal that he would forgive the last months charge and that he was being very gracious in doing so.  I again told him I didn't even have a gateway to use the service and that T-Mobile had it after sending me the return email and label. He again said it was never fully canceled to their satisfaction whatever that means.  We went round and round until I said I would pay $55 for the month I disputed even though I had canceled the service just to end the madness. He said he also insisted on the $25. I said no and that I wanted to only pay $55 only on that day.  He said it would cost an additional $10 to have an operator take my payment.  I asked if I could do it online without a charge and he said yes but it would not give me a confirmation.  Because he was not helpful at all we ended the call.  I went home and paid $55 online and still received a confirmation number. It seemed that Patrick was trying to squeeze even more money from me. The amount of time and effort I have put into resolving this is ridicules. There appears to be no training for customer service either at the store level or at corporate.
Update 10/15/23  T-Mobile reached out and we reached a mutual understanding.

for starters they suspended my account and when i contacted them they said it was “accidental” and unsuspended it. not to bad but then i couldn’t change my plan or view any other plans in app. i opened a triuble ticket and nothing happened so i was stuck with my call/text unlimited only. Ranks at the very bottom of my experiences over the past 55 years with companies.  Three weeks ago, we contacted T-Mobile about upgrading our cell phones to newer models.
